failure_modes.text: Evaluators can protect favored theories or people instead of using the standard to falsify their assumptions.
failure_modes.source_quote: extreme-performance-evaluation conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when evaluators protect favored hypotheses instead of falsifying them.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the standard to falsify assumptions, not to protect favored theories or people; evaluation integrity depends on willingness to let the data disappoint.

failure_modes.text: Score maximization can push teams too close to operating limits when the evaluation logic ignores necessary buffers.
failure_modes.source_quote: extreme-performance-evaluation conflicts with margin-of-safety (margin of safety) when score-maximization pushes teams to run too close to operational limits.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build buffers into the evaluation logic so score-maximization does not push the team past safe operating limits.

failure_modes.text: Extreme standards can dissolve into fear or noise when context, support, and measurement quality do not match the standard being imposed.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when extreme standards are imposed without matching context, support, or measurement quality, turning evaluation into noise, fear, or vanity metrics.
failure_modes.mitigation: Match context, support, and measurement quality to the standard being imposed; extreme standards dissolve into fear when the infrastructure does not support them.

premortem_questions.text: How much of the observed performance is skill signal, and how much is variance or luck?
premortem_questions.source_quote: separate signal from luck across difficult, nonlinear, or high-variance performance environments

premortem_questions.text: What safety margin are we quietly sacrificing in order to maximize the score?
premortem_questions.source_quote: run too close to operational limits

premortem_questions.text: Would this standard still be useful if the team did not know which candidate we already favored?
premortem_questions.source_quote: protect favored hypotheses instead of falsifying them

heuristics.text: Use the evaluation to distinguish robust excellence from acceptable noise, not to reward surface effort.
heuristics.source_quote: distinguish robust excellence from merely acceptable output

heuristics.text: Check whether the standard has enough measurement quality and support behind it to be interpretable.
heuristics.source_quote: matching context, support, or measurement quality

heuristics.text: Keep a margin-of-safety lens in the review so extreme performance does not become reckless performance.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on separating excellence from luck and appearance rather than on generic performance-management language.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
